description: Oracle test facts for conservation laws in Hamiltonian mechanics. Tests knowledge of symplectic geometry, Liouville theorem, and specific invariants.

facts.context: For an autonomous Hamiltonian system with H(q, p), the quantity H is:
facts.truth: conserved along trajectories (dH/dt = 0 via Hamilton's equations)

facts.context: Liouville's theorem states that for Hamiltonian systems:
facts.truth: phase space volume is preserved under time evolution

facts.context: The mathematical structure that underlies Liouville's theorem is:
facts.truth: symplectic geometry - the 2-form ω = Σ dq_i ∧ dp_i is preserved

facts.context: The first Poincaré integral invariant ∮ p dq is:
facts.truth: conserved for any loop in phase space evolving under Hamiltonian flow

facts.context: Noether's theorem relates conservation laws to:
facts.truth: continuous symmetries of the Lagrangian (time → energy, space → momentum, rotation → angular momentum)

facts.context: For the Kepler problem (gravitational two-body), angular momentum L is conserved because of:
The Laplace-Runge-Lenz vector A = p × L - μm r̂ is conserved for:
facts.truth: the Kepler problem only (unique to 1/r potential, reflects hidden SO(4) symmetry)

facts.context: Conservation of the Laplace-Runge-Lenz vector implies:
facts.truth: closed elliptical orbits that don't precess (orbits return to same point)

facts.context: For an integrable Hamiltonian system, action variables J_i = (1/2π) ∮ p_i dq_i are:
facts.truth: adiabatic invariants that remain constant even under slow parameter changes

facts.context: The KAM theorem for nearly integrable Hamiltonian systems states:
facts.truth: most invariant tori survive small perturbations (chaos doesn't immediately destroy all structure)

facts.context: The Hénon-Heiles system H = (px² + py²)/2 + (x² + y²)/2 + x²y - y³/3 is:
facts.truth: non-integrable and exhibits chaos at high energies (only energy is globally conserved)

facts.context: For a chaotic Hamiltonian system, Liouville's theorem implies:
facts.truth: phase space volume is still conserved even though trajectories are chaotic
distractors: Liouville's theorem doesn't apply to chaos

facts.context: A canonical transformation (q, p) → (Q, P) preserves:
facts.truth: the symplectic 2-form and Poisson brackets ({Q_i, P_j} = δ_ij)

facts.context: Generating functions for canonical transformations are related to:
facts.truth: Hamilton-Jacobi theory and the principal function S(q, α, t)

facts.context: A Hamiltonian system with n degrees of freedom is completely integrable if it has:
facts.truth: n independent integrals of motion in involution (Liouville-Arnold theorem)

facts.context: The Poisson bracket {F, H} of a quantity F with the Hamiltonian gives:
facts.truth: the time derivative dF/dt (F is conserved iff {F, H} = 0)
